what_is_interesting: Zhuangzi's most famous paradox — he dreamed he was a butterfly and upon waking could not tell if he was a man who had dreamed of being a butterfly, or a butterfly now dreaming of being a man — makes him the perfect guide to dissolve the boundary between waking and sleep.
likely_image: A lone robed figure on a misty mountain path or beneath a gnarled tree, lit by moonlight, in a dark dreamlike ink-wash or atmospheric Asian landscape.
winner_reasoning: Against a misty moonlit Taoist figure, 'STILL DREAMING' conjures Zhuangzi's butterfly paradox in present tense — are you already asleep? are you ever truly awake? — making the viewer feel the philosophy before they click, and promising exactly the dissolving drift the video delivers.
